title: Logic Manipulation
wikitext:
[[File:Venuzdonoa Dodge.gif|thumb|[[Venuzdonoa]] possesses the power to destroy Reason itself, allowing it's wielder to cut someone even if they dodged the attack.|400x400px]]

== Summary ==
'''Logic Manipulation''' is the power to alter, overcome, or negate the fundamental principles of logic and rationality that govern existence and perception. This ability allows users to create scenarios that defy normal understanding, enabling contradictions or nonsensical reasoning to function within reality. The possible effects cover a wide range of alterations of reality’s fundamental rules.

It should be noted that manipulating "Logic" in the form of altering the [[Tiering System FAQ#Q: When does a set of all possible worlds qualify for High 1-A+?|logical basis of all possible worlds]] is an ability exclusive to Tier '''0''' entities, however lesser forms of the ability which affect a smaller scope still can and do appear in fiction.
== Defining Logic ==
To justify the existence of Logic Manipulation as a separate ability instead of being appended to an ability like [[Law Manipulation]], and in order to prevent [https://vsbattles.fandom.com/wiki/Fallacies#15._Equivocation equivocation fallacies], it is necessary to provide a rigorous definition of what qualifies as "logic", because fiction is littered with [[Hyperbole|hyperbolic]] statements of characters "violating logic", and a verse may function off of alternative systems that are not rooted in [[wikipedia:Classical_logic|classical logic]].

Logic is [[wikipedia:Logic|defined]] in the real world as the study of correct reasoning. Translating this to fiction, manipulation of logic would essentially involve manipulating the rules governing [[wikipedia:Deductive_reasoning|deductive reasoning]]. Such rules provide the [[wikipedia:Grounding_(metaphysics)|grounding]] for any deductive inference about the world, and thus for any and all intelligibility whatsoever. As such, they are not, and cannot be, rooted in any [[Law Manipulation|laws]] more fundamental than themselves. Rather, they are the basis for any and all [[Law Manipulation|laws]], [[Mathematics Manipulation|calculation]], and [[Causality Manipulation|relations]] between phenomena.

As should be obvious by now, simple statements of characters "doing the impossible" or "breaking logic" are not nearly enough to qualify for this ability. Characters must be shown to be capable of directly interfering with a form of deductive reasoning that tangibly governs their verse's reality, and acts as the grounding for intelligibility within the verse.
== Possible Uses ==
Logic Manipulation can be applied in diverse ways, including:
* '''Logic Creation:''' A user might override formal logical laws or rational rules, allowing them to establish alternate principles. This could include introducing new, previously invalid [[wikipedia:Rule_of_inference|rules of inference]], or replacing fundamental laws like the [[wikipedia:Law_of_excluded_middle|law of excluded middle]] or the [[wikipedia:Law_of_identity|law of identity]] with other laws.
* '''Logic Deletion:''' A user might erase a logical law without replacing it. An example would be erasing the [[wikipedia:Law_of_noncontradiction|law of non-contradiction]], making it so that phenomena can be and not be simultaneously without restriction.
* '''Paradox Creation:''' The user can create and maintain scenarios that are self-contradictory or paradoxical, for example enabling mutually exclusive states to exist simultaneously, such as an object being both present and absent at once. Contrary to Logic Deletion they may keep the laws as they are, but allow the things they manipulate to be exceptions to those rules.
== Limitations ==
By its very nature Logic Manipulation can, with sufficient evidence, pose an exception to virtually any rule we have. In exchange, Logic Manipulation is much more tightly bound to exactly what it is said and shown to do. As Logic Manipulation by its very nature violates deductive reasoning, one can only do a very limited amount of such reasoning based on it. Typically one is limited directly to the explanations of the verse regarding what it can do and can not extrapolate any further, even if seemingly sensible.

For example, it might be tempting to say that a logic manipulator who is explained to be able to manipulate logic to change reasoning such that being burned does not alter his state of being, would be able to do a similar thing to defend against water-based attacks. However, for a logic-breaking power, there is no true guarantee that it would abide by such a seemingly sensible pattern and the principle of explosion would indeed prevent such reasoning.

If the explanation of the power, on the other hand, clearly states that the user is able to do something like that against any attack, then it would be fine to operate under the assumption that it does so.
== Caveats ==
*The ability for one to exist in a logic-violating state themself should be listed as [[Paraconsistent Physiology]] and not Logic Manipulation. Logic Manipulation only covers the ability to directly alter the logic exterior of oneself.
*Alterations to logic should only be listed as Logic Manipulation if truly of a fundamentally logical nature. Changes such as making 1 + 1 = 3 should for instance be listed as [[Mathematics Manipulation]] instead, as that is only a logical contradiction if one assumes the axioms of mathematics to be valid. For similar reasons, time paradoxes get listed as [[Acausality]] or [[Causality Manipulation]] and are not fundamentally considered logical contradictions.
*[[Toon Force]] also does not qualify, as while the actions one can perform are often absurd and may be called "illogical", they are not usually achieved through actual manipulation of logic.
== Users ==
* [[Venuzdonoa]]
